implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001 dispatch_specialist_stage exists and returns StageDispatchResult
AC-002 refuses non-specialist stages with ValueError
AC-004 delegates with capability matching stage
AC-005 correlation_id threaded into dispatch envelope
AC-006 stage_log entry written on submit + updated on reply with Coach payload
AC-008 unit tests cover success, degraded, soft-timeout paths

completion_promises.criterion_text: dispatch_specialist_stage(stage: StageClass, build_id: str, ...) -> StageDispatchResult function exists at src/forge/pipeline/dispatchers/specialist.py
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented async dispatch_specialist_stage(stage=, build_id=, correlation_id=, forward_context_builder=, dispatch_surface=, stage_log_writer=, feature_id=None, attempt_no=1, retry_of=None) returning StageDispatchResult dataclass at src/forge/pipeline/dispatchers/specialist.py. TestSurface verifies isinstance(result, StageDispatchResult).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Refuses any stage that is not in {PRODUCT_OWNER, ARCHITECT} (raises ValueError -- programming error, not runtime)
completion_promises.evidence: Function refuses early with ValueError naming the offending stage when stage not in SPECIALIST_CAPABILITY_BY_STAGE (which keys on PRODUCT_OWNER and ARCHITECT only). TestStageRefusal parametrises across the six other StageClass members and verifies (a) ValueError is raised, (b) no dispatch is attempted, (c) no stage_log row is written.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Calls ForwardContextBuilder.build_for to assemble the dispatch payload context
completion_promises.evidence: dispatch_specialist_stage calls forward_context_builder.build_for(stage=stage, build_id=build_id, feature_id=feature_id) and threads each returned ContextEntry into the DispatchParameter list as 'context' params. TestForwardContextWiring verifies the architect dispatch carries the product-owner approved charter text in its parameter values, and that the product-owner dispatch (entry stage) carries no forward context.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Delegates to FEAT-FORGE-003's specialist-dispatch tool with the capability matching the stage (product_owner_specialist, architect_specialist)
completion_promises.evidence: SPECIALIST_CAPABILITY_BY_STAGE maps PRODUCT_OWNER -> 'product_owner_specialist' and ARCHITECT -> 'architect_specialist'; dispatcher passes the mapped capability to dispatch_surface.dispatch(). TestCapabilityRouting parametrises both stages and asserts surface.last_call['capability'] equals the expected name and that SPECIALIST_CAPABILITY_BY_STAGE[stage] matches.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Threads the build's correlation_id through the dispatch envelope (Group I @data-integrity scenario)
completion_promises.evidence: _build_dispatch_parameters always emits a non-sensitive 'correlation_id' DispatchParameter as the first entry, value threaded unchanged from the function argument. TestCorrelationIdThreading asserts exactly one such parameter exists, value matches input verbatim, and sensitive=False so persistence does not redact it.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Records the dispatch as a stage_log entry on submit and updates on reply with the parsed Coach score and detection findings
completion_promises.evidence: Defined StageLogWriter Protocol with two methods: record_dispatch_submit (returns entry_id) and record_dispatch_reply. Dispatcher calls submit before delegating to FEAT-FORGE-003 and reply after translating the outcome, threading coach_score / criterion_breakdown / detection_findings / reason. TestStageLogLifecycle asserts (a) one submit row with capability + correlation_id, (b) one reply row carrying the parsed Coach score (0.91) + criterion breakdown + findings keyed to the same entry_id, and (c) write-before-send: an observing dispatch surface sees one submit row already present at dispatch time.

completion_promises.criterion_text: On degraded specialist (no healthy specialist on cache), returns a StageDispatchResult.DEGRADED outcome -- gating layer maps to flag-for-review (Group C @negative scenario 'no product-owner specialist')
completion_promises.evidence: _translate_outcome maps Degraded -> StageDispatchOutcome.DEGRADED, copying outcome.reason through. StageDispatchResult re-exports the enum members as ClassVar attributes so AC-007's literal access pattern (StageDispatchResult.DEGRADED) works. TestDegradedPath seeds a Degraded reply with reason='no_specialist_resolvable' and asserts result.outcome is StageDispatchResult.DEGRADED and the writer records the same outcome + reason.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit test mocking FEAT-FORGE-003 dispatch surface: success path, degraded path, soft-timeout path (retry-with-context)
completion_promises.evidence: Three dedicated test classes use the in-memory RecordingDispatchSurface fake (which structurally satisfies SpecialistDispatchSurface, asserted in TestProtocolConformance): TestSuccessPath (SyncResult -> COMPLETED with coach_score=0.78), TestDegradedPath (Degraded -> DEGRADED), TestSoftTimeoutPath (DispatchError(error_explanation='local_timeout') -> SOFT_TIMEOUT, plus a non-timeout error variant -> ERROR). TestSoftTimeoutPath also asserts the dispatcher does NOT retry (call_count == 1) -- retry-with-context lives at the supervisor layer per ASSUM-005.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran `ruff check` on src/forge/pipeline/dispatchers/specialist.py, src/forge/pipeline/dispatchers/__init__.py, and tests/forge/pipeline/dispatchers/test_specialist.py -- 'All checks passed!'. Ran `black --target-version py312` -- 2 files left unchanged after reformatting test file. Final pytest: 24/24 new tests pass; full repo regression `pytest tests/forge/ --ignore=tests/forge/adapters/test_specialist_dispatch.py` -> 1263 passed, 0 failed.
